content.text:
<think>
Looking at the chart "Can I offer you a drink?" showing percent of total alcohol consumption by type (1965-2015):

- **Legend**: beer=orange, spirits=blue/teal, wine=red/pink, other=small
- Examining all 10 countries (Australia, Austria, Belgium, Brazil, Canada, Chile, China, Costa Rica, Cuba, Czechia)

Costa Rica stands out clearly: the spirits (blue) area is very high (~70-80% of consumption) from 1965 through approximately 1995, then drops dramatically and steeply to roughly 40% over just a few years around 1995-2000. This cliff-like drop over roughly 5 years is far more abrupt than any other country:
- Chile shows a gradual multi-decade decline
- China has a slow decrease over many years
- Cuba shows fluctuations but no single massive drop
- Other countries have gradual or minor changes

The Costa Rica chart shows an almost vertical drop in the blue (spirits) band around 1995, making it the most dramatic short-period decline.
</think>

<answer>
Costa Rica shows the most dramatic drop in spirits consumption in a very short period of time. The spirits percentage (blue band) was very high (~70-80%) from 1965 through about 1995, then dropped sharply and steeply over just a few years around 1995-2000, falling to roughly half its previous level in what appears as an almost vertical decline on the chart.
</answer>

<think>
Looking at the chart "Can I offer you a drink?" showing percent of total alcohol consumption by type (1965-2015):

- **Legend**: beer=orange, spirits=blue/teal, wine=red/pink, other=small
- Examining all 10 countries (Australia, Austria, Belgium, Brazil, Canada, Chile, China, Costa Rica, Cuba, Czechia)

Costa Rica stands out clearly: the spirits (blue) area is very high (~70-80% of consumption) from 1965 through approximately 1995, then drops dramatically and steeply to roughly 40% over just a few years around 1995-2000. This cliff-like drop over roughly 5 years is far more abrupt than any other country:
- Chile shows a gradual multi-decade decline
- China has a slow decrease over many years
- Cuba shows fluctuations but no single massive drop
- Other countries have gradual or minor changes

The Costa Rica chart shows an almost vertical drop in the blue (spirits) band around 1995, making it the most dramatic short-period decline.
</think>

<answer>
Costa Rica shows the most dramatic drop in spirits consumption in a very short period of time. The spirits percentage (blue band) was very high (~70-80%) from 1965 through about 1995, then dropped sharply and steeply over just a few years around 1995-2000, falling to roughly half its previous level in what appears as an almost vertical decline on the chart.
</answer>
